name: Farrell–Jones Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Farrell–Jones Conjecture as a closed categorical statement: K*(BG) ≅ K*(EG ×_G R) for all groups G and rings R

VINIT.justification: Before any assembly map is applied, K-theory exists as pure potential—uninitialized, awaiting the morphism that will structure it

TANCH.justification: The closed boundary that contains the entire system: the statement that assembly maps induce isomorphisms on K-theory and L-theory for all groups

AFWD.chosen_element: Assembly map μ: K*(EG ×_G R) → K*(BG)
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism from the equivariant K-theory (computed via the universal cover EG) toward the group ring K-theory (target space BG)

AREV.chosen_element: Inverse assembly map (conjectured): K*(BG) → K*(EG ×_G R)
AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: the conjectured inverse direction, descent from group ring K-theory back to equivariant K-theory, completing the isomorphism

CLINK.chosen_element: Composition of assembly maps across subgroups
CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: the assembly map for a subgroup H ⊆ G composes with the map for G, forming a tower of K-theory computations

IMSCRIB.justification: K-theory recognizes itself: the functor K* is natural and self-consistent across all group rings and classifying spaces—identity of the entire system

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Splitting into K-theory and L-theory branches
FSPLIT.justification: The Farrell–Jones Conjecture branches into two distinct paths: K-theory (algebraic K-groups) and L-theory (quadratic forms / surgery obstruction), each requiring separate assembly maps

FFUSE.chosen_element: Reunification of K and L-theory via the Farrell–Jones isomorphism
FFUSE.justification: Both K-theory and L-theory branches reconstitute the original conjecture: if both assembly maps are isomorphisms, the full Farrell–Jones statement is proven

EVALT.chosen_element: Assembly map is an isomorphism (success state)
EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: the conjecture holds for this group/ring pair; K*(BG) ≅ K*(EG ×_G R) is proven true

EVALF.chosen_element: Assembly map fails to be an isomorphism (failure state)
EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: the conjecture is false for this group/ring pair; there exists a counterexample where the assembly map is not bijective

ENGAGR.chosen_element: Undecidable case: assembly map status unknown
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice state: for certain exotic groups or rings, the assembly map's injectivity and surjectivity are simultaneously held as open questions—both true and false, unresolved

IFIX.chosen_element: Proven cases: assembly map verified for specific groups (e.g., finite groups, hyperbolic groups, CAT(0) groups)
IFIX.justification: Permanent, append-only record: each verified case is irreversibly fixed in the mathematical literature; once proven, it cannot be undone

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: Farrell–Jones Conjecture branches into K-theory and L-theory
phase_2.split_input: The Farrell–Jones Conjecture (unified statement about assembly maps)
split_outputs: K-theory assembly map: μ_K: K*(EG ×_G R) → K*(BG)
L-theory assembly map: μ_L: L*(EG ×_G R) → L*(BG)
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: Reconstitution of Farrell–Jones via isomorphism of both branches
phase_2.fuse_result: The Farrell–Jones Conjecture (both assembly maps are isomorphisms, restoring the original unified statement)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — Uninitialized K-theory: before any group G or ring R is specified, before any assembly map is constructed
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Assembly map is an isomorphism: the Farrell–Jones Conjecture holds for this (G, R) pair; K-theory computation succeeds
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Assembly map is not an isomorphism: the conjecture fails; there exists a counterexample or obstruction
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Paradice state: the assembly map's status is simultaneously open and closed, proven and unproven, for exotic or undecidable cases

steps.domain_action: Uninitialized K-theory functor, void state before any group or ring is named
The Farrell–Jones Conjecture as closed boundary: assembly maps are isomorphisms for all groups and rings
Assembly map μ: K*(EG ×_G R) → K*(BG) — forward morphism from equivariant to group ring K-theory
Composition of assembly maps across subgroup towers H ⊆ G — sequential chaining of K-theory computations
K-theory as self-referential functor — the system recognizes itself through naturality and consistency
Branch into K-theory and L-theory — the conjecture splits into two distinct assembly map problems
K-theory assembly map is an isomorphism — affirmative evaluation for K-theory branch
Forward morphism within K-theory branch — computation proceeds toward the target K*(BG)
K-theory verified for finite groups — permanent record: assembly map proven bijective for finite G
K-theory verified for hyperbolic groups — append-only: assembly map proven bijective for hyperbolic G
K-theory verified for CAT(0) groups — irreversible: assembly map proven bijective for CAT(0) G
L-theory assembly map is an isomorphism — affirmative evaluation for L-theory branch
Reverse morphism within L-theory branch — descent from surgery obstruction back to equivariant L-theory
L-theory verified for finite groups — permanent record: assembly map proven bijective for finite G
L-theory verified for virtually polycyclic groups — append-only: assembly map proven bijective
Reconstitute Farrell–Jones Conjecture from K-theory and L-theory branches — both assembly maps are isomorphisms
Farrell–Jones Conjecture as self-referential closed loop — the system recognizes its own truth
Undecidable case: assembly map status unknown for exotic groups — paradice: both true and false held simultaneously
Assembly map fails for certain exotic groups — negative evaluation: counterexample found or obstruction proven
Reverse morphism: attempt to recover the conjecture from failure — descent into the negation
Composition of failed cases — sequential chaining of counterexamples and obstructions
Counterexample recorded — permanent, irreversible: assembly map is not bijective for this group
Boundary holds: Farrell–Jones Conjecture remains the closed statement, whether proven or disproven

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Algebraic K-theory functor K*: translates group rings RG and classifying spaces BG into abelian groups K_n(RG) and K_n(BG)
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Natural transformations and commutative diagrams: assembly maps communicate between equivariant K-theory K*(EG ×_G R) and group ring K-theory K*(BG)
phase_5.memory_mechanism: K-theory groups K_n and L-theory groups L_n: state is stored as abelian group structures; classifying spaces BG and universal covers EG serve as persistent reference
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Inductive structure on group towers: computation proceeds from subgroups H to larger groups G, ordered by inclusion; assembly maps are applied in sequence
phase_5.alfs_store: The Farrell–Jones Conjecture itself: canonical reference and bootstrap store for all K-theory and L-theory computations; all assembly maps are measured against this statement

phase_6.cycle_cost: Computational complexity of K-theory calculation: each assembly map application requires computing K*(EG ×_G R) and K*(BG), which grows with group complexity
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Uncomputed K-theory: K*(BG) and K*(EG ×_G R) exist as abstract functors, not yet evaluated
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Computed K-theory: assembly map μ is applied, yielding either an isomorphism (EVALT) or a failure (EVALF); state is fixed in the mathematical record
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The Farrell–Jones Conjecture is entropy-conservative: the assembly map is a natural transformation that preserves the categorical structure; no information is lost or created, only reorganized through the isomorphism

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → IFIX → IFIX → IFIX → EVALT → AREV → IFIX → IFIX → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → EVALF → AREV → CLINK → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Farrell–Jones Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(11,2,4,6)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=23
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 15)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [8] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [9] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [12] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [13] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [14] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [15] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [16] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [17] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑖  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [18] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑳 → 𐑗  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [19] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑖 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [20] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [21] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [22] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def farrell_jones_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Uninitialized K-theory functor, void state before any group or ring is named)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The Farrell–Jones Conjecture as closed boundary: assembly maps are isomorphis...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Assembly map μ: K*(EG ×_G R) → K*(BG) — forward morphism from equivariant to ...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Composition of assembly maps across subgroup towers H ⊆ G — sequential chaini...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (K-theory as self-referential functor — the system recognizes itself through n...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch into K-theory and L-theory — the conjecture splits into two distinct assembly map problems) / FFUSE [15] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (6 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (K-theory assembly map is an isomorphism — affirmative evaluation for K-theory...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism within K-theory branch — computation proceeds toward the tar...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (K-theory verified for finite groups — permanent record: assembly map proven b...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (K-theory verified for hyperbolic groups — append-only: assembly map proven bi...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (K-theory verified for CAT(0) groups — irreversible: assembly map proven bijec...)
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (L-theory assembly map is an isomorphism — affirmative evaluation for L-theory...)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [12] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse morphism within L-theory branch — descent from surgery obstruction ba...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [13] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (L-theory verified for finite groups — permanent record: assembly map proven b...)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [14] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (L-theory verified for virtually polycyclic groups — append-only: assembly map...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [15]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [15] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute Farrell–Jones Conjecture from K-theory and L-theory branches — both assembly maps are isomorphisms)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [16] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Farrell–Jones Conjecture as self-referential closed loop — the system recogni...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑖)  -- [17] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Undecidable case: assembly map status unknown for exotic groups — paradice: b...)
  (.arrow 𐑖 𐑳 𐑗)  -- [18] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Assembly map fails for certain exotic groups — negative evaluation: counterex...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑖 𐑱)  -- [19] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse morphism: attempt to recover the conjecture from failure — descent in...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑭)  -- [20] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Composition of failed cases — sequential chaining of counterexamples and obst...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [21] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Counterexample recorded — permanent, irreversible: assembly map is not biject...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [22] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Boundary holds: Farrell–Jones Conjecture remains the closed statement, whethe...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def farrell_jones_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (farrell_jones_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def farrell_jones_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (farrell_jones_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem farrell_jones_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
